```diff
+status/logs, use the `cloudflare-api` MCP tool against
+`/accounts/{accountId}/pages/projects/begbie-hugo/deployments` rather than the dashboard.
```

1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## What this is
6	
7	A Hugo static site for Rod Begbie's personal/coaching site (`coaching.begbie.com`), using a
8	vendored copy of the **portio** theme (Bootstrap 5.3.8). Deployed to Cloudflare Pages via `wrangler`.
9	
10	## Commands
11	
12	```bash
13	hugo server -D        # local dev server with drafts, live reload
14	hugo                   # build to public/ (generated, gitignored)
15	```
16	
17	Hugo version is pinned to **0.163.3** — set via `HUGO_VERSION` in `wrangler.jsonc`, which is what
18	Cloudflare Pages reads to pick the build image. There is no package.json, Makefile, or test suite;
19	content/template correctness is verified by building and checking `public/` or the dev server.
20	
21	## Architecture
22	
23	**Theme is vendored, not a submodule.** `themes/portio` is a full copy of the theme committed
24	directly into this repo (see commit `55bde54`, "Copy in the portio theme, instead of submoduling
25	it"). `.gitmodules` exists but is empty — don't expect `git submodule update` to do anything.
26	Theme edits happen directly under `themes/portio/layouts`; there is no project-level `layouts/`
27	override directory.
28	
29	**Homepage is built from toggleable, data-driven sections.** Each section partial in
30	`themes/portio/layouts/partials/` (e.g. `aboutSection.html`, `heroSection` via `hero.html`,
31	`resumeSection.html`, `serviceSection.html`, `skillSection.html`, `portfolioSection.html`,
32	`testimonialSection.html`, `blogSection.html`) reads its content from a same-named YAML file in
33	`data/` (e.g. `data/aboutSection.yml`). Every data file starts with `enable: true|false`, and the
34	partial wraps itself in `{{ if .enable }}` — flip that flag to show/hide a section, no template
35	changes needed. Currently disabled: `portfolioSection`, `serviceSection`, `skillSection`.
36	
37	**Data is read via `hugo.Data.<name>`, not `.Site.Data.<name>`.** This was changed in commit
38	`d4d2e42` ("Fix Hugo compatibility for modern versions") when upgrading to Hugo 0.157 — `.Site.Data`
39	is deprecated and `resources.ToCSS` was replaced with `css.Sass`. If you add a new data-driven
40	section or touch SCSS compilation, follow these same patterns rather than older portio-theme
41	examples found online (`themes/portio/exampleSite` still uses the old API and is reference-only,
42	not built).
43	
44	**Design tokens live in `themes/portio/assets/scss/_tokens.scss`.** CSS custom properties
45	(`--accent`, `--ink`, `--bg`, etc.) plus Sass variables for fonts/spacing/radii. New components
46	should reference these, not hardcode hex/px values.
47	
48	**Decorative blob backgrounds use `layouts/partials/blob.html`**, called as
49	`{{ partial "blob.html" (dict "id" "unique-gradient-id" "color" "var(--accent)" "stopOpacity" ".08") }}`
50	inside a `.blob-wrap` wrapper div. One shared partial for every section's blob — don't hand-copy
51	the inline SVG again.
52	
53	**Résumé data is split into `recent`/`earlier` lists**, not one flat list sliced by `first N`.
54	`data/resumeSection.yml`'s `recent` entries render as cards; `earlier` entries generate the
55	"Earlier: ..." summary line directly in the template. Add a new job by moving the oldest `recent`
56	entry into `earlier`, not by editing prose by hand.
57	
58	**Content types:**
59	- `content/blog/*.md` — blog posts (front matter: `title`, `date`, `featureImage`,
60	  `featureImageAnchor` (optional, overrides smart-crop anchor — see below), `subtitle`), rendered
61	  through `themes/portio/layouts/blog/{list,single}.html`.
62	- There is no `/contact/` page — removed (was unlinked from nav/hero/footer, its form posted to an
63	  unconfigured Formspree placeholder). Site contact is the homepage's `#contact` footer CTA
64	  (mailto link) or `data/aboutSection.yml`/`data/hero.yml` links.
65	- Everything else on the homepage (`hero`, `about`, `resume`, `testimonials`, etc.) is YAML in
66	  `data/`, not Markdown content.
67	
68	**Static verification files** like `static/.well-known/apple-developer-merchantid-domain-association`
69	are opaque payloads required by third parties (Apple Pay) — don't reformat or "clean up" their
70	contents.
71	
72	## Gotchas
73	
74	**Clear the Hugo cache before testing image changes.** `rm -rf public resources/_gen
75	.hugo_build.lock` before `hugo`/`hugo server` when iterating on `.Fill`/`.Resize` calls — Hugo
76	caches processed image variants keyed by the processing-spec string, and stale cached files can
77	mask whether an edit actually took effect.
78	
79	**Responsive images use width-descriptor srcset + `sizes`, not `1x`/`2x`.** Bootstrap 5's grid
80	makes `.row > *` full-width by default below the column's breakpoint (e.g. `col-lg-4` is 100%
81	wide below `lg`), so images/thumbnails often render *larger* on mobile/tablet than in a desktop
82	multi-column layout — the opposite of the naive assumption. Compute real per-breakpoint CSS widths
83	from the actual column math before picking `sizes` breakpoints for any new responsive image.
84	
85	**`featureImageAnchor` front matter** (e.g. `Left`, `TopLeft`, `Center`) overrides Hugo's default
86	`smart` crop anchor for a post's `featureImage`, applied via
87	`.Params.featureImageAnchor | default "smart"` in both the thumbnail and banner `Fill` calls.
88	
89	**Hugo template scoping inside nested `with`/`range`:** `.` becomes the resource (not the page)
90	inside `{{ with resources.Get ... }}`, and `$` still refers to the template's original entrypoint
91	context (not the loop item) inside `{{ range }}` — capture `{{ $post := . }}` before entering
92	nested scopes if you need page fields inside them.
93	
94	**JSON-LD in `<script>` tags needs `| safeJS`** after `| jsonify` — otherwise Go's `html/template`
95	autoescaper treats the bare text as a JS string literal and double-encodes the whole blob.
96	
97	**Cloudflare Rocket Loader** (zone-level, `coaching.begbie.com` only — not `*.pages.dev` previews)
98	rewrites `<script>` `type` attributes, breaking any script not marked `data-cfasync="false"`. If JS
99	works on a PR preview but not production, check this before assuming a code regression.
100	
101	**`gh pr review --approve` always fails** with "Can not approve your own pull request" for this
102	repo/account — expected, not a bug. Skip straight to `gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch`.
103	
104	**`git add path1 path2 ...` aborts entirely if any path doesn't exist** (common after `git mv`/`rm`
105	leaves a path already staged) — no files get staged, not just the missing one. Run `git status`
106	first and pass only currently-existing paths.
107	
108	**SVG `<stop>` elements need `style="stop-color: var(--x)"`, not `stop-color="var(--x)"`.** The
109	bare presentation attribute doesn't resolve CSS custom properties — only the `style` attribute
110	goes through the CSS cascade. Applies to any gradient referencing a design token.
111	
112	**Passing a variable into `style="..."` in a Go template trips the CSS-context auto-escaper** —
113	`style="stop-color: {{ $color }}"` silently renders literal `ZgotmplZ` instead of the value if
114	`$color` isn't a compile-time string literal. Build the string with `printf` and pipe through
115	`| safeCSS`: `style="{{ printf "stop-color: %s;" $color | safeCSS }}"`. Always grep the built
116	`public/` output for `ZgotmplZ` after adding a partial with dynamic inline styles — it fails
117	silently, no build error.
118	
119	**Bootstrap's `$utilities` map is trimmed to only what's currently used** (see
120	`themes/portio/assets/scss/_utilities-trim.scss`) — down from Bootstrap's ~50
121	default utility categories to the 11 this site actually uses (`display`,
122	`justify-content`, `align-items`, `margin-top`/`-end`/`-bottom`/`-x`/`-y`,
123	`padding`, `text-align`, `color`), each restricted to only its in-use values.
124	`$grid-breakpoints`/`$container-max-widths` are similarly trimmed to
125	`xs`/`sm`/`md`/`lg` (`xl`/`xxl` unused). If a template needs a stock Bootstrap
126	utility class not already covered (e.g. `mt-3`, `bg-primary`, `rounded-pill`),
127	it silently resolves to no CSS — not a build error. Add the missing key/value
128	to `_utilities-trim.scss` rather than assuming any Bootstrap utility class
129	will work out of the box. The `forms` and `pagination` Bootstrap SCSS modules
130	are no longer imported either (zero/near-zero usage) — re-add the import in
131	`style.scss` if a future page actually needs form controls or paginated
132	listings.
133	
134	**`position: relative` alone does NOT create a stacking context.** A child with `z-index: -1`
135	needs its positioned ancestor to also declare `z-index` (even `z-index: 0`), or the negative
136	z-index escapes to a page-wide shared layer *below every section's own background* — including
137	sections with an opaque background that then paint over it, making the child invisible with no
138	error. Every section that wraps a decorative blob (`.hero`, `.about_content-thumb`,
139	`.blog-preview`, `.breadCrumb`, `.singleBlog__feature`, `.footer`) sets `position: relative;
140	z-index: 0;` together for this reason — keep them paired in any new section.
141	
142	**External links get `target="_blank"` via a render hook, not `unsafe = true`.**
143	`themes/portio/layouts/_default/_markup/render-link.html` compares the link's host against
144	`site.BaseURL` and adds `target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"` only for external links.
145	Don't re-enable `markup.goldmark.renderer.unsafe` in `config.toml` — it disables HTML
146	sanitization for every markdown field on the site to solve a problem this hook already covers.
147	
148	**Hugo's `smart` crop anchor can clip a portrait's headroom.** For hero/about-style headshots,
149	`Top`-anchored `.Fill` calls are a safer default than `smart` when the subject's hair/head must
150	stay fully visible — `smart` optimizes for overall visual interest, not for "don't cut off the
151	top of the frame."
152	
153	## Deployment

content.text:
Both pages build and load cleanly. Summary of the fix:

**Root cause:** the `sizes` attribute on blog thumbnails and the blog banner used stale breakpoints (`1200px`/`1400px`) that don't correspond to the site's actual trimmed grid (`xs/sm/md/lg` only, capped at `lg: 992px`, container maxing at 1280px). That mismatch made the browser think the image would render much wider than it actually does, so it picked the largest (900w) srcset candidate even for a 332px-wide card.

**Fix — recomputed from the real grid math** (container: 1280px max, 40px padding; column gutter: 24px):
- `blogCard.html` (3-up grid ≥768px, stacked <768px):
  `(min-width: 1280px) 376px, (min-width: 768px) calc((100vw - 152px) / 3), calc(100vw - 104px)`
- `blog/single.html` banner (always full-width `col-lg-12`):
  `(min-width: 1280px) 1176px, calc(100vw - 104px)`

Built and spot-checked — no `ZgotmplZ`, correct `sizes` present in the generated HTML for both the homepage blog cards and single-post page.

One thing I *didn't* touch: `hero.html`/`aboutSection.html` have the same stale-breakpoint pattern in their `sizes` strings, and on closer look they actually risk the *opposite* problem in the 768–991px range (undersized, not oversized) — worth a follow-up if you want it, but it wasn't what Lighthouse flagged here so I left it alone.

Want me to branch/PR this?
Create a branch. Then Let's fix the hero/about ones too, and open a PR once they're fixed.
